assertions.title: Registration page renders and creates account

steps.expected: Login form with Register now link
steps.observed: Login form displayed with Register now link visible
Navigated to /register, form displayed with Email or Phone, Password, Confirm Password fields and Register button

steps.expected: Account created, redirect to login or auto-login
steps.observed: Account created, auto-logged in and redirected to home page (http://localhost:3100/)

evidence.network: POST /api/auth/register -> account created, auto-login redirect to /

steps.action: Clear cookies to start fresh unauthenticated session
Fill form with test@example.com / password123 (existing account)

steps.expected: Error message shown, stay on register page
steps.observed: Error message 'Account already exists with this email or phone' displayed in red, user remains on /register

assertions.title: Logout clears session and redirects

steps.action: Navigate to My tab via bottom navigation
steps.expected: My page displayed with user profile and Logout button
steps.observed: My page displays only 'My Profile' heading and 'User profile coming soon...' placeholder text. No logout button present.

steps.expected: Logout button visible on My page
steps.observed: No logout button exists anywhere on the My page or in the entire codebase. The My page (app/my/page.tsx) is a bare placeholder with no user info or logout functionality.

assertions.issues: The My page is a bare placeholder ('User profile coming soon...') with no logout button. No signOut/logout functionality is implemented anywhere in the codebase. The validation contract requires a Logout button on the My page that clears the session and redirects to login.

frictions.description: After successful registration (VAL-AUTH-008), the user is auto-logged in and redirected to home page instead of being redirected to login page. The validation contract says 'redirects to login or auto-logs in' so this is acceptable behavior.
frictions.resolution: Auto-login after registration is an acceptable alternative per the contract wording.

frictions.description: To test VAL-AUTH-009 (duplicate registration), had to clear cookies first since the previously registered user was auto-logged in and the app redirects authenticated users away from /register.
frictions.resolution: Cleared cookies via agent-browser cookies clear to access /register as unauthenticated user.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-AUTH-008 registration, VAL-AUTH-009 duplicate error), 1 failed (VAL-AUTH-012 logout - My page is a placeholder with no logout button implemented).
